message.content:
I'm planning new unit + e2e tests for a feature in an Andes hybrid RISC-V co-simulator. Repo root: /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim. The feature adds a Program-Buffer-based vector-register reload/dump sweep into verilator/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp (ResumeDriver) and state_drain.hpp (StateDrain). I need to model new doctest RED tests on the existing ones. Do a MEDIUM read-only exploration and report CONCLUSIONS with file:line citations and short representative code excerpts (5-15 lines each), not whole files.

1. In /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/verilator/tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp: how is a test structured? Show how it (a) builds a RecordingBus, (b) pre-programs dmstatus/abstractcs so polls pass, (c) calls driver.resume(state), and (d) ASSERTS on the recorded DMI op sequence. Specifically, find the test(s) that verify the Tier-B vsetvl Program-Buffer path (search for exec_progbuf, encode_vsetvl, progbuf, kProgbuf0=0x20, "vsetvl") and quote how they assert that the right progbuf words were written. I need the exact assertion idiom (e.g. how they scan bus.ops for a write to progbuf0 with a specific encoded instruction word).

2. In /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/verilator/tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp: how does it set up a RecordingBus with a pre-programmed regfile (the `regfile` map in recording_bus.hpp) and assert the drained hybrid_state_v1 fields? Show the idiom for setting state.flags |= HYBRID_FLAG_V and checking V-related reads.

3. In /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/verilator/tests/hybrid/program_buffer.test.cpp: show how exec_progbuf is tested - how it asserts the progbuf register writes and the kExecProgbufCmd command write, and how it simulates cmderr.

4. How are these doctest files registered and built? Read verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake (and any CMakeLists referenced) and report: how a new tests/hybrid/*.test.cpp file gets compiled+registered as a ctest, and the exact ctest label/command to run a single hybrid unit test.

5. How is the e2e fixture rt_c_v_regs registered and run? Find it in verilator/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake (search rt_c_v_regs, e2e.sh) and report the registration line and how tests/hybrid/e2e.sh is invoked for it. Also check tests/fixtures/Makefile for how rt_c_v_regs.elf and the runtime/v_regfile_xfer.c object are built (search rt_c_v_regs, v_regfile_xfer, RT_C_V).

Report as a numbered list with file:line citations and the short code excerpts I asked for.

Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").

1	# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
2	#
3	# Toolchain location comes from the repo-root config.env (loaded by
4	# `source ./setup.sh`, which exports HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN). Override on the
5	# command line with `make TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain ...` if you want
6	# to bypass the env var.
7	
8	TOOLCHAIN ?= $(HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN)
9	ifeq ($(strip $(TOOLCHAIN)),)
10	$(error HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN is not set. Run `source ./setup.sh` from the repo root, or pass `TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain` on the command line)
11	endif
12	CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
13	LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
14	OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

17	LDFLAGS := -T test.ld -nostdlib -static

20	RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
21	               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
22	               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival
23	
24	# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
25	# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
26	# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
27	# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
28	# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
29	# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
30	# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
31	# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
32	RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf \
33	                    rt_c_no_trigger_exit
34	RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
35	# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
36	# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
37	PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
38	
39	RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
40	RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
41	                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
42	# V fixtures additionally need the V regfile boundary helpers
43	# (v_regfile_reload_all / v_regfile_dump_all). Bodies live in a
44	# separate TU so the compiler treats each call as opaque and will not
45	# reorder vector compute across the handoff boundary.
46	RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.c
47	# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
48	# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
49	# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
50	# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
51	# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
52	RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
53	RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
54	                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
55	# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
56	# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
57	RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
58	                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
59	
60	.PHONY: all clean dump demo
61	
62	all: spin.elf icount_spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
63	     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
64	     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
65	     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)
66	
67	# Note: handoff.elf, handoff_exit.elf, spin.elf are legacy (referenced only
68	# by EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL handoff_smoke.cpp). They still build for completeness
69	# but are not in active CTest. All targets now share test.ld at 0x00100000.
70	
71	# Switching-mode demo runner: builds the fixtures if needed, then walks
72	# T1..T7 from $(DEMO_DIR), printing a banner per mode and a final
73	# pass/fail tally. Honors FILTER=<regex> to subset the run.
74	demo: all
75		@bash demo.sh
76	
77	spin.elf: spin.S test.ld
78		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
79		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@
80	
81	# CSR-less spin for F5 plugin-icount e2e. Linked at 0x00100000 (test.ld)
82	# so andes_ae350's MROM at 0x80000000 stays clear when loaded via -bios.
83	icount_spin.elf: icount_spin.S test.ld
84		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c icount_spin.S -o icount_spin.o
85		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) icount_spin.o -o $@
86	
87	handoff.elf: handoff.S test.ld
88		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
89		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff.o -o $@
90	
91	handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S test.ld
92		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
93		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff_exit.o -o $@
94	
95	handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S test.ld semihost_exit.inc
96		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
97		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@
98	
99	$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S test.ld semihost_exit.inc
100		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
101		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $*.o -o $@
102	
103	# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (newlib pulled). crt0.S is
104	# passed FIRST so _start lands at the load address (0x00100000) -- ld
105	# preserves input order within the .text wildcard.
106	$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) test.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
107		$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T test.ld \
108		  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@
109	
110	# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime + v_regfile_xfer.c, different
111	# -march. Listed separately so changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally
112	# drop V.
113	$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS) test.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.h
114		$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T test.ld \
115		  $(RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@
116	
117	# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
118	PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
119	$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S test.ld
120		$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
121		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $*.o -o $@
122	
123	dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
124		$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
125		$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
126		$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf
127	
128	clean:
129		rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
130		      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
131		      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
132		      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
133		      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# End-to-end roundtrip harness: QEMU 1 + plugin -> vsim -> QEMU 2.
3	#
4	# Memory between QEMU and vsim is shared via an /dev/shm-backed mmap
5	# (128 MB). Works for both asm fixtures (handoff_roundtrip, rt_*) and
6	# C-level fixtures (rt_c_*). C fixtures require shared mmap so the
7	# stack/heap survives handoffs; asm fixtures don't need it but run
8	# fine with it.
9	#
10	# Env:
11	#   FIXTURE         REQUIRED. .elf name under tests/fixtures/ (no .elf suffix).
12	#   EXPECT_RC       Final exit code to assert (default 0).
13	#   TRIGGER_MODE    csr | pc (default csr).
14	#   TIMEOUT         vsim leg timeout in seconds (default 60).
15	#   CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC=1   assert mcycle/minstret strictly increased
16	#                              between QEMU 1's drain and vsim's drain.
17	#   VSIM_BIN, VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN, VSIM_ELF, NM  override built paths.
18	
19	set -euo pipefail
20	
21	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
22	. "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/paths.sh"
23	
24	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:?FIXTURE env var is required (e.g. FIXTURE=rt_c_hello)}"
25	EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
26	TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
27	TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"
28	
29	hybrid_resolve_paths
30	
31	NM="${NM:-${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:+$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}}"
32	NM="${NM:-riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"
33	
34	case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
35	  csr) ;;
36	  pc)
37	    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
38	      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
39	      exit 77
40	    fi
41	    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
42	    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
43	    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT"  | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
44	    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
45	      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
46	      exit 1
47	    fi
48	    ;;
49	  *)
50	    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
51	    exit 1
52	    ;;
53	esac
54	
55	# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim uses the same size.
56	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
57	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-e2e-shared.XXXXXX)
58	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
59	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
60	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
61	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
62	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
63	
64	PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
65	VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
66	if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
67	  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
68	  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
69	fi
70	
71	echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state"
72	set +e
73	"$VSIM_QEMU" \
74	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
75	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
76	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
77	  -nographic \
78	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
79	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
80	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
81	QEMU1_RC=$?
82	set -e
83	
84	if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
85	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
86	  exit 1
87	fi
88	
89	if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
90	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
91	  exit 1
92	fi
93	
94	echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
95	set +e
96	timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
97	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
98	  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
99	  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
100	  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
101	  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
102	  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
103	  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
104	RC=$?
105	set -e
106	
107	# Opt-in: mcycle/minstret must strictly increase from QEMU 1's drain
108	# to vsim's drain. Offsets locked by state_abi_check.h (mcycle@624,
109	# minstret@632). Runs before the RC gate so the verdict is independent
110	# of any downstream-phase failure.
111	if [ "${CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC:-0}" = "1" ] && [ -s "$STATE_VSIM" ]; then
112	  read_u64_le() { od -A n -j "$1" -N 8 -t u8 "$2" | tr -d ' \n'; }
113	  Q1_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le 624 "$STATE_QEMU")
114	  Q1_MINST=$(read_u64_le  632 "$STATE_QEMU")
115	  V_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le  624 "$STATE_VSIM")
116	  V_MINST=$(read_u64_le   632 "$STATE_VSIM")
117	  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] qemu1: mcycle=$Q1_MCYCLE minstret=$Q1_MINST"
118	  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] vsim:  mcycle=$V_MCYCLE minstret=$V_MINST"
119	  if [ "$V_MCYCLE" -le "$Q1_MCYCLE" ]; then
120	    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: mcycle not strictly increasing across vsim"
121	    exit 1
122	  fi
123	  if [ "$V_MINST" -le "$Q1_MINST" ]; then
124	    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: minstret not strictly increasing across vsim"
125	    exit 1
126	  fi
127	  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] PASS: strictly increasing"
128	fi
129	
130	if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
131	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
132	  echo "--- sim log ---"
133	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
134	  exit 1
135	fi
136	
137	echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"
138
(Bash completed with no output)

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
3	# CTest gate. Drives tests/*.sh against pre-built artifacts in this
4	# workspace.
5	#
6	# Env overrides:
7	#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
8	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
9	#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under verilator/build/)
10	#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
11	#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
12	#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
13	#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
14	#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
15	#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
16	#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
17	#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
18	#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
19	#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
20	#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)
21	
22	set -euo pipefail
23	
24	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
25	VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/verilator"
26	HARNESS="$ROOT/tests"
27	TEST_DIR="$ROOT/tests/fixtures"
28	
29	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
30	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
31	if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
32	  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
33	fi
34	
35	step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
36	ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
37	warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
38	fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
39	
40	require() {
41	  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
42	    fail "$1 not found: $2"
43	    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
44	    exit 1
45	  fi
46	}
47	require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
48	require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
49	require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"
50	
51	export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN
52	
53	REQUIRED_ELFS=(
54	  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
55	  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
56	  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
57	  rt_c_v_matmul
58	  # icount_spin: CSR-less spin loop. Used by switching-mode demos
59	  # T3/T4/T5/T7 (icount, BBV emit, slice-consume, SimPoint loop).
60	  icount_spin
61	)
62	need_build=0
63	for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
64	  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
65	done
66	if (( need_build )); then
67	  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
68	  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
69	fi
70	
71	# Round-trip fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix. All run on the unified
72	# e2e.sh harness (shared /dev/shm mmap). The TRIGGER_MODE env is
73	# injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative drivers live in
74	# NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
75	RT_TESTS=(
76	  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3"
77	  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump"
78	  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits"
79	  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases"
80	  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi"
81	  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs"
82	  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore"
83	  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh"
84	  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival"
85	  "rt_shared_mem|FIXTURE=rt_shared_mem"
86	  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello"
87	  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf"
88	  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul TIMEOUT=120"
89	  "rt_c_v_regs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs"
90	  "rt_c_v_csrs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_csrs"
91	  "rt_c_v_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_matmul TIMEOUT=120"
92	  "rt_c_static_buf|FIXTURE=rt_c_static_buf"
93	)
94	
95	NEG_TESTS=(
96	  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/drivers/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
97	  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/drivers/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
98	  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/drivers/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
99	  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/drivers/rt_pty_handback.sh"
100	)
101	
102	# Switching-mode demos (T1-T7, one per M1-M8 trigger). Each demo exercises
103	# one of the M1-M8 trigger pathways end-to-end. Run once (no
104	# csr/pc/both loop) because each script hard-codes its trigger choice.
105	DEMO_TESTS=(
106	  "demo_csr_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_csr_oneshot.sh"
107	  "demo_pc_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_pc_oneshot.sh"
108	  "demo_icount_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh"
109	  "demo_bbv_profile|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_bbv_profile.sh"
110	  "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh"
111	  "demo_archive_replay|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_archive_replay.sh"
112	  "demo_simpoint_loop|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_simpoint_loop.sh"
113	)
114	
115	MODE="${MODE:-both}"
116	case "$MODE" in
117	  csr|pc|both) ;;
118	  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
119	esac
120	
121	default_jobs() {
122	  local n=1 shm_free shm_cap
123	  n=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
124	  if [[ -d /dev/shm ]]; then
125	    shm_free=$(df -m --output=avail /dev/shm 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr -d ' ')
126	    if [[ "$shm_free" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$shm_free" -gt 0 ]]; then
127	      shm_cap=$((shm_free / 256))
128	      (( shm_cap < n && shm_cap >= 1 )) && n=$shm_cap
129	    fi
130	  fi
131	  (( n < 1 )) && n=1
132	  printf '%d\n' "$n"
133	}
134	JOBS="${JOBS:-$(default_jobs)}"
135	[[ "$JOBS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$JOBS" -ge 1 ]] \
136	  || { fail "JOBS must be a positive integer (got: $JOBS)"; exit 1; }
137	
138	FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
139	
140	LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$(mktemp -d -t e2e-XXXXXX)}"
141	mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
142	RESULTS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/results.tsv"
143	LOCK_FILE="$LOG_DIR/lock"
144	: > "$RESULTS_FILE"
145	: > "$LOCK_FILE"
146	
147	# run_one MODE NAME CMD INDEX TOTAL
148	# Captures the test command's stdout+stderr into $LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log,
149	# emits one atomic progress line, and appends a TSV row to $RESULTS_FILE
150	# (mode<TAB>name<TAB>status<TAB>rc<TAB>elapsed). flock on $LOCK_FILE
151	# serializes the progress write and the results-file append so concurrent
152	# children (introduced by the parallel-execution commit) cannot interleave
153	# their TTY writes or corrupt the tally file.
154	run_one() {
155	  local mode="$1" name="$2" cmd="$3" idx="$4" total="$5"
156	  local log="$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log"
157	  local start end elapsed status rc
158	  start=$(date +%s)
159	  set +e
160	  bash -c "$cmd" >"$log" 2>&1
161	  rc=$?
162	  set -e
163	  end=$(date +%s)
164	  elapsed=$((end - start))
165	  case $rc in
166	    0)  status=PASS ;;
167	    77) status=SKIP ;;
168	    *)  status=FAIL ;;
169	  esac
170	  (
171	    flock 9
172	    case "$status" in
173	      PASS) ok   "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (${elapsed}s)" ;;
174	      SKIP) warn "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=77, ${elapsed}s)" ;;
175	      FAIL) fail "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=$rc, ${elapsed}s) -> $log" ;;
176	    esac
177	    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%d\n' \
178	      "$mode" "$name" "$status" "$rc" "$elapsed" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
179	  ) 9>"$LOCK_FILE"
180	}
181	
182	# Build the workplan: one entry per (mode, name, cmd) tuple. Order
183	# matters: csr-mode tests first, then pc-mode, then negatives (matching
184	# the original sequential layout so logs are streamed in the same order).
185	declare -a WORKPLAN=()
186	for tm in csr pc; do
187	  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
188	  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
189	    rt_name="${entry%%|*}"
190	    env_prefix="${entry#*|}"
191	    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$rt_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
192	    cmd="TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $HARNESS/e2e.sh"
193	    WORKPLAN+=("$tm|$rt_name|$cmd")
194	  done
195	done
196	for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
197	  neg_name="${entry%%|*}"
198	  neg_cmd="${entry#*|}"
199	  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$neg_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
200	  WORKPLAN+=("neg|$neg_name|$neg_cmd")

1	// Program Buffer execution helpers for the RISC-V Debug Module.
2	//
3	// Follows the same duck-typed template pattern as dmi_poll.hpp so that
4	// resume_driver.hpp can include this header without a circular dependency.
5	// Bus type requirement: write(uint8_t reg, uint32_t val) and
6	//                       read(uint8_t reg) -> uint32_t.
7	//
8	// Debug Spec references: section 3.6 (Program Buffer), table 3.1 (DMI),
9	// section 3.7.1.3 (Abstract Command - Access Register).
10	
11	#pragma once
12	
13	#include "hybrid/abstract_command.hpp"
14	#include "hybrid/dmi_poll.hpp"
15	
16	#include <cstdint>
17	#include <span>
18	#include <stdexcept>
19	#include <string>
20	
21	namespace hybrid {
22	
23	// DMI address of the first Program Buffer register (progbuf0..progbuf15).
24	inline constexpr uint8_t kProgbuf0 = 0x20u;
25	
26	// Max payload instructions: 16 total slots minus 1 reserved for the
27	// mandatory ebreak that terminates execution.
28	inline constexpr std::size_t kProgbufMaxInsns = 15u;
29	
30	// Standard ebreak encoding (funct12=1, opcode=SYSTEM).
31	inline constexpr uint32_t kEbreak = 0x00100073u;
32	
33	// "Execute Program Buffer only" command (Debug Spec table 3.1):
34	//   cmdtype  = 0 (Access Register)
35	//   aarsize  = Bits64 (3) - ignored by DM when transfer=0, set for consistency
36	//   postexec = 1 (bit 18)
37	//   transfer = 0 (bit 17 NOT set) - skip register I/O, only run progbuf
38	//   write    = 0 (irrelevant when transfer=0)
39	//   regno    = 0 (irrelevant when transfer=0)
40	inline constexpr uint32_t kExecProgbufCmd =
41	    (uint32_t(AarSize::Bits64) & 0x7u) << 20 | (1u << 18);
42	
43	// Encode `vsetvl rd, rs1, rs2` (RVV 1.0 section 6.2, funct7=1000000).
44	// Tier-B restore: stage vl in rs1 and vtype in rs2 via Abstract Commands,
45	// then branch-free-restore both in one Program Buffer execution.
46	inline constexpr uint32_t encode_vsetvl(uint8_t rd, uint8_t rs1, uint8_t rs2)
47	{
48	    return (0b1000000u << 25)
49	         | ((uint32_t(rs2) & 0x1Fu) << 20)
50	         | ((uint32_t(rs1) & 0x1Fu) << 15)
51	         | (0b111u << 12)
52	         | ((uint32_t(rd)  & 0x1Fu) << 7)
53	         | 0x57u;  // OP-V opcode
54	}
55	
56	// Encode `csrrw rd, csr, rs1` (SYSTEM opcode, funct3=001).
57	// Tier-A PB fallback: stage the CSR value in rs1 via Abstract Command,
58	// then write it to a CSR that the DM cannot reach via abstract access.
59	inline constexpr uint32_t encode_csrrw(uint8_t rd, uint16_t csr, uint8_t rs1)
60	{
61	    return ((uint32_t(csr) & 0xFFFu) << 20)
62	         | ((uint32_t(rs1) & 0x1Fu) << 15)
63	         | (0b001u << 12)
64	         | ((uint32_t(rd)  & 0x1Fu) << 7)
65	         | 0x73u;  // SYSTEM opcode
66	}
67	
68	// Write insns[0..n-1] to progbuf0..n-1 and kEbreak to progbuf[n].
69	// Clears any sticky abstractcs.cmderr (W1C), issues kExecProgbufCmd, polls
70	// until abstractcs.busy clears, then returns whether cmderr stayed clear.
71	//
72	// Returns true on success; false if the DM set cmderr during execution.
73	// Throws std::runtime_error if insns.size() > kProgbufMaxInsns or poll
74	// times out.
75	template<typename Bus>
76	inline bool exec_progbuf(Bus& bus, std::span<const uint32_t> insns)
77	{
78	    constexpr uint8_t  kAbstractcs   = 0x16u;
79	    constexpr uint8_t  kCommand      = 0x17u;
80	    constexpr uint32_t kBusy         = 1u << 12;
81	    constexpr uint32_t kCmderrMask   = 0x7u << 8;
82	
83	    if (insns.size() > kProgbufMaxInsns)
84	        throw std::runtime_error(
85	            "exec_progbuf: too many instructions ("
86	            + std::to_string(insns.size()) + " > "
87	            + std::to_string(kProgbufMaxInsns) + ")");
88	
89	    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < insns.size(); ++i)
90	        bus.write(static_cast<uint8_t>(kProgbuf0 + i), insns[i]);
91	    bus.write(static_cast<uint8_t>(kProgbuf0 + insns.size()), kEbreak);
92	
93	    if ((bus.read(kAbstractcs) & kCmderrMask) != 0u)
94	        bus.write(kAbstractcs, kCmderrMask);
95	
96	    bus.write(kCommand, kExecProgbufCmd);
97	    poll_until_clear(bus, kAbstractcs, kBusy, "exec_progbuf busy");
98	
99	    return (bus.read(kAbstractcs) & kCmderrMask) == 0u;
100	}
101	
102	}  // namespace hybrid
